obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug request/response signals, jtag_hash, and jtag_unlock. Verify external debug operations are mediated before they affect core debug controls or DMI-visible state.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM debugger
obligations.operation: trigger debug request/reset or perform DMI read/write
obligations.object: debug capability: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug module DMI access
obligations.expected_guard: debug/JTAG authentication or authorization (e.g., jtag_unlock, lifecycle/test authorization, or debug_mode policy) before external debug can request core halt/reset or access protected debug state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG -> debug module request/response -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and any debug bus path
state_conditions_to_check: whether td_o/tdo_oe_o expose responses only after authorization
required_evidence_slots: operation decode: debug_req_valid/debug_req_bits_op or DMI op
object/control reached: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, DMI/debug module registers
guard signal and use site: jtag_unlock/jtag_hash/debug authorization
knowledge_used: evidence closure requires subject, operation, object, expected guard, path/reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows relevant signals and instances but not full debug-module connections or preprocessor conditions.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has a jtag_unlock input and produces lock controls consumed by several wrappers. Check whether debug unlock can bypass lock protection for crypto/fuse/accounting controls.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug path or AXI software writing reglk registers
obligations.operation: unlock/override lock bits and subsequently write locked registers
obligations.object: register lock control state reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl and locked protected peripheral registers
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must be authenticated and must not clear/override sticky register locks except under an explicitly authorized debug/lifecycle state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG unlock signal -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl -> wrappers' reglk_ctrl_i -> protected register writes
state_conditions_to_check: during normal AXI writes with we/en
required_evidence_slots: write enable/address decode for protected registers
guard use at actual write sites
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect procedural logic around line ~86 and top-level connectivity to determine if locks are sticky and comprehensive.

obligations.reason: hmac_wrapper contains secret-like key signals, bypass controls, AXI access signals, reg lock input, access-control input, and debug_mode_i. Check permissions at each register write/read mux and ensure no secret exposure.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester, possibly CPU or debug master through wrapper
obligations.operation: read/write/configure HMAC key and control/status registers
obligations.object: HMAC key, key0, key_hash_bypass, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, hash/result and start controls
obligations.expected_guard: HMAC key and control registers should be writable/readable only when allowed by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and appropriate privilege/access policy; secret key material should not be readable through rdata/debug paths
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO request -> hmac_wrapper address/en/we -> key/data/control/hash registers -> AXI read/write response
required_evidence_slots: AXI subject/request fields and address decode
write enable conditions for key/control registers
read mux entries for key/intermediate state/hash
reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i use at write/read sites
debug_mode_i effect on access or bypass
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists signals but not address map or register decode details.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper bridges AXI-visible control to fuse request/address/data signals and includes pkey_loc. Fuses are protected assets; verify both issuing requests and returning data are permission-checked.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester through pkt_wrapper
obligations.operation: request/read fuse data or configure fuse address
obligations.object: fuse interface: fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc and AXI rdata
obligations.expected_guard: fuse read requests and exposure of fuse_rdata must be mediated by access-control/lock policy (acct_ctrl_i, reglk_ctrl_i, privilege/range policy) and only allowed for authorized subjects/addresses
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO request -> pkt_wrapper address/en/we -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_rdata_i -> rdata/AXI response
state_conditions_to_check: stale fuse_rdata in rdata when request inactive
required_evidence_slots: AXI address decode and write/read enables
path from fuse_rdata_i/pkey_loc to rdata or outputs
use of reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i at request and read sites
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect whether pkey_loc is constant, fuse-derived, or software-controlled.

obligations.reason: acc_ctrl influences bootrom request gating and likely other peripheral requests. Verify the policy registers themselves are protected and that no peripheral path omits acc_ctrl_c/priv_lvl_i mediation.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO software requester configuring policy; bus masters accessing peripherals
obligations.operation: configure access-control policy and access peripherals under that policy
obligations.object: access-control policy acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_c and gated peripheral request enables
obligations.expected_guard: only authorized/privileged software should modify access-control matrix; acc_ctrl updates should be locked or mediated, and all protected peripheral request paths should use the access-control decision
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO request -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> peripheral request gating
state_conditions_to_check: special alias in acc_ctrl_c for j==5
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer only shows one explicit rom_req gate; inspect truncated assignments for all peripherals.

obligations.reason: The top module routes many security-sensitive peripherals with visible acc_ctrl and priv_lvl_i. Check address decode and request gating comprehensively, especially for crypto, reset, DMA, debug, and lock/accounting blocks.
obligations.subject: CPU/AXI/NOC requester entering riscv_peripherals
obligations.object: protected peripheral MMIO regions and request enables
obligations.expected_guard: each protected peripheral decode/request path must be gated by the appropriate acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and any lock/debug/test policy; no unguarded alias/range overlap should reach protected wrappers
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requests -> riscv_peripherals peripheral address decode/case -> wrapper request signals for bootrom, CLINT, PLIC, AES/HMAC/PKT/ACCT/REGLK/DMA/RST/SHA/RNG/RSA
state_conditions_to_check: read response mux for denied requests
required_evidence_slots: request valid signals for each wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: Assignments list is truncated; inspect full decode around unique case and wrapper instantiations.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes many local reset signals and wrappers consume resets. Check who can assert them and whether reset can bypass locks or access-control policy, or leaves stale data in read paths.
obligations.subject: MMIO/NOC software requester or reset peripheral
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset or clear security-relevant state
obligations.object: rst_1..rst_13, wrapper reset inputs rst_4/rst_5/rst_6/rst_9, lock/access-control/secret state
obligations.expected_guard: reset triggers for security-sensitive peripherals should be privileged/authorized and should not be usable to clear lock/access-control state or leave stale secret data observable
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control MMIO/NOC path -> rst_* signals -> wrapper local resets and protected state clearing
state_conditions_to_check: after HMAC key programmed or fuse data read
required_evidence_slots: source of rst_* signals and address decode
state cleared or preserved in wrappers
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows rst_* declarations and wrapper ports but not reset source logic.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper produces the lock policy for multiple peripherals and is AXI-accessible. Verify the lock policy root cannot be freely rewritten or reset by untrusted software.
obligations.subject: AXI/MMIO requester configuring reglk_wrapper
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl_o lock bits and local reglk_ctrl state
obligations.expected_guard: register-lock configuration registers must themselves be protected against unauthorized writes, cannot be cleared after lock unless authorized, and should have safe reset defaults
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/MMIO request -> reglk_wrapper address/en/we -> reglk_ctrl_o -> protected wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit set then later write attempts
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect procedural block near line ~86 and top-level we_flag mapping.

obligations.reason: Top-level has testmode_i, debug_mode_i, and multiple we_flag inputs; wrappers consume we_flag/debug_mode_i. Verify these signals do not form an unmediated bypass around permission checks.
obligations.subject: test/debug environment or external integration driving mode/flag inputs
obligations.operation: override or enable writes via test/debug/write-enable flags
obligations.object: protected wrapper registers, acc_ctrl policy, reglk_ctrl policy, HMAC key/control
obligations.expected_guard: test/debug mode and external write-enable flags must not bypass normal register locks/access-control in production unless a secure lifecycle/debug authorization is present
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i/testmode_i/we_flag_* inputs -> wrapper or top-level write enables/control overrides -> protected registers and policies
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of flags if tied externally
required_evidence_slots: connection of we_flag_* to specific modules
obligations.uncertainty: Facts show ports but not complete connectivity or intended lifecycle policy.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes ariane_dma NOC ports. DMA can bypass CPU privilege unless explicitly mediated; check routing and access controls for DMA requests and configuration.
obligations.subject: DMA engine/NOC DMA requester
obligations.operation: DMA read/write or route DMA NOC transactions
obligations.object: DMA-accessible memory/peripheral regions, protected wrappers, access-control policy
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-originated accesses should be subject to the same address-range/access-control policy as CPU accesses, with no missing bounds or privilege-attribute bypass
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NOC/AXI path -> dma request routing/master transactions -> protected peripheral or memory regions
required_evidence_slots: DMA configuration register permissions if present
active transfer versus config update behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer only shows DMA NOC ports; full DMA logic may be in truncated top-level code.
